name: Abd al-A'la ibn Yazid
title: Companion of Muslim
shortDescription: A brave companion of Muslim ibn Aqeel who took up arms to assist him in Kufa and was martyred by Ibn Ziyad.
fullStory:
Abd al-A'la ibn Yazid al-Kalbi was a brave and militant Shia in the city of Kufa. When Muslim ibn Aqeel rose up in Kufa and Ibn Ziyad dispersed the people through threats and intimidation, Abd al-A'la was one of the few who stood firm with all his might.

He left his neighborhood armed to rush to Muslim's aid, but was arrested by the forces of Kathir ibn Shihab (one of Ibn Ziyad's agents). They took him to the Dar al-Imara before Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad.

Ibn Ziyad violently asked him why he had drawn his weapon. Abd al-A'la, a pious and courageous man, did not hide the truth. Ibn Ziyad ordered him to be imprisoned.

Shortly after, to instill fear among the people of Kufa, Ibn Ziyad ordered Abd al-A'la to be brought out of prison and beheaded in an area called 'Jabbana al-Sabi'. He sacrificed his life to help the Imam's ambassador.
quote: (In response to Ibn Ziyad:) I went out to assist Muslim ibn Aqeel.
